title: Understanding the Forces
text: Andrea's force is directed due north, while Jennifer's force is at an angle of $$32$$ degrees east of north. To find the resultant direction, we need to consider both the magnitude and direction of these forces.

title: Breaking Down the Forces
text: To find the resultant direction, break down Jennifer's force into its northward and eastward components. Use trigonometric functions to do this.

title: Using Trigonometry
text: The northward component of Jennifer's force can be found using cosine: $$180cos\left(32\right)$$. The eastward component can be found using sine: $$180sin\left(32\right)$$.

title: Calculating the Resultant Force
text: Add Andrea's force to Jennifer's northward component to get the total northward force. The eastward component remains the same as Jennifer's eastward component.

title: Finding the Direction
text: Use the tangent function to find the angle of the resultant force: tan(theta) $$=$$ eastward component / northward component.

title: Calculate the Angle
text: Calculate the angle using the arctangent function: theta $$=$$ arctan(eastward component / northward component).
